src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-data-sync-001 title: verify firebase data synchronization for new user registration module: data synchronization source files or pages: 1 covered methods: preconditions: - firebase database is accessible and empty for test data. - local user interface is open and connected to the data organizer. - no existing users in the system. test data: - valid user: `nickname="testuser123"`, `password="securepass123!"`, `pos_lat=40.7128`, `pos_lon=-74.0060`, `tokens=0`, `rate=0.0` - invalid user: `nickname=""`, `password="weak"`, `pos_lat=999.999`, `pos_lon=-999.999` test steps: 1. user enters valid credentials in the local user interface. 2. system attempts to register the user via data organizer → firebase. 3. verify firebase database now contains the user record with correct attributes. 4. repeat step 1 with invalid credentials (empty nickname, weak password, invalid coordinates). 5. system rejects registration and displays error: "invalid input. check nickname, password, and location." expected result: - valid user is successfully synced to firebase with all attributes. - invalid user registration fails; error message appears locally. - firebase database remains unchanged for invalid attempts. unit: project:

src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-chat-002 title: verify message deletion and relation cleanup module: chat functionality source files or pages: 2, 3 covered methods: preconditions: - a chat exists with 2 messages between usera and userb. - firebase contains the `chat`, `message`, and `relation` records. test data: - message to delete: `id=201`, `chat_id=1`, `text="hello!"` - relation to verify: `chat=1`, `user="usera"` test steps: 1. usera deletes message `id=201` via local user → data organizer. 2. data organizer sends `rimuoviprodotto()`-style request to firebase to delete the message. 3. verify message `id=201` is removed from firebase. 4. check if the `chat` record remains intact (not deleted). 5. userb refreshes the chat and confirms the message is gone. expected result: - message is deleted from firebase without affecting the chat or other messages. - chat record persists; no cascading deletions occur. - userb’s ui reflects the deletion after sync. unit: project:
